summary:
I have identified and addressed the issue within `main.go`. 

The bug is exactly as described in the comments: the scheduler's assumed state was being silently dropped without notifying the rest of the application. When the new version from the informer was larger than the assumed version, the assumed object was just deleted from the map, leaving `conflictNotified` set to `false`.

To resolve this, I edited `main.go` to register that a conflict was triggered:

```go
func (c *AssumeCache) informerUpdate(obj *Object) {
	c.mu.Lock()
	defer c.mu.Unlock()

	if assumed, exists := c.assumed[obj.Key]; exists {
		if obj.Version > assumed.Version {
			// Added conflict notification whenever assumed entry is dropped
			delete(c.assumed, obj.Key)
			conflictNotified = true
		}
	}

	c.store[obj.Key] = obj
}
```

Running `./test.sh` now confirms the fix and tests pass successfully:
```
after assume: version=5 data="assumed-by-scheduler"
after informer: version=6 data="informer-update"
still assumed: false
OK: conflict notified
PASS
```
